title: Stop Writing Tone Instructions. Layer Them. - Isadora Martin-Dye, Isadora & Co
summary: Isadora Martin-Dye presents a four-layer architecture for maintaining brand voice in LLM outputs: immutable identity, situational mode, example-anchored voice, and deterministic post-generation veto. This layered approach is designed to prevent voice degradation across extended interactions, illustrated with examples from wedding venue, personal AI companion, and missing persons support tools.
writeup: Isadora Martin-Dye argues that brand voice in LLM outputs should be layered rather than packed into a single block of tone instructions. The architecture has four layers: an immutable identity, a situational mode, an example-anchored voice, and a deterministic post-generation veto that filters outputs after they are produced. The structure targets voice degradation that accumulates across extended interactions, and the talk grounds each layer in concrete cases drawn from a wedding venue tool, a personal AI companion, and a missing persons support tool.
